agent_breakdown_fast_narrative: moves fast. Good segment to prioritize in client communication, urgency framing and pricing conversations.
agent_breakdown_balanced_narrative: looks balanced. Use it as a reference segment where pricing and marketing quality matter a lot.
agent_breakdown_slow_narrative: moves slower. This may be a segment where stronger positioning, better presentation or pricing adjustments help.
buyer_breakdown_negotiation_narrative: may give buyers more room to negotiate. Inventory is not disappearing especially fast.
buyer_breakdown_competitive_narrative: looks more competitive. Good listings may move faster, so preparation and financing readiness matter more.
developer_breakdown_strong_narrative: shows relatively strong absorption. This can be a good candidate for deeper land, zoning and pricing analysis.
developer_breakdown_weak_narrative: is not showing exceptional absorption right now. It may still work, but it needs stronger local validation.
agent_usage_advice: Use this segment to decide where to focus attention, how much urgency to communicate, and whether pricing should be aggressive or more flexible.
buyer_usage_advice: Use this segment to estimate your negotiation position. Higher DOM and weaker removal-to-new usually give you more time and more leverage.
developer_usage_advice: Use this as a first screening layer. Stronger absorption and faster velocity can justify deeper work on land, planning, pricing and pipeline assumptions.
agent_next_checks:
Compare this segment with the same city but different estate type.
Check whether pricing per m² is rising or just supply is shrinking.
Review fastest listings inside this segment and see what product profile wins.
buyer_next_checks:
Compare this segment against a slower one to see where negotiation may be easier.
Look at the slowest listings and identify patterns: price, condition, location, floor, building type.
Use DOM together with new supply to decide whether waiting helps or hurts.
developer_next_checks:
Validate demand with price trend, not velocity alone.
Compare primary vs secondary if market type data exists.
Use the fastest segments as candidates for deeper feasibility and land search.
fastest_listing_description: This listing belongs to the fastest-moving group.
slowest_listing_description: This listing belongs to the slowest-moving group.
agent_fast_listing_narrative: This kind of listing likely matches what the market accepts quickly. Treat it as a benchmark for pricing, product profile and client urgency.
agent_slow_listing_narrative: This listing stayed longer on the market. It may reveal where pricing, product quality, location or presentation weakens demand.
buyer_fast_listing_narrative: Listings like this may require quicker decisions. If you target similar homes, prepare financing and criteria earlier.
buyer_slow_listing_narrative: Listings like this can help spot negotiation opportunities. Check whether the longer exposure is caused by price, condition or location.
developer_fast_listing_narrative: This profile may indicate stronger take-up in this micro-segment. Useful as a signal for further pipeline or product-fit analysis.
developer_slow_listing_narrative: This profile may show weaker fit or oversupply. Useful when stress-testing what not to build or how to differentiate.
agent_fast_next_step: Compare it with your active listings and check what features match: city, type, market type and price level.
agent_slow_next_step: Check comparable slower listings and see whether one recurring weakness appears.
buyer_fast_next_step: Save similar listings and track how quickly they disappear.
buyer_slow_next_step: Review price history and compare with newer alternatives.
developer_fast_next_step: Compare this profile with top-performing segments in the same city.
developer_slow_next_step: Check whether slow movement is structural or only local to this product type.

metric_median_dom_agent: Median DOM tells you how fast similar listings disappear. Lower DOM usually means stronger demand and less time to react.
metric_removed_30d_agent: Removed 30d shows how many listings left the market recently. Higher values often mean stronger transaction activity or stronger demand.
metric_new_30d_agent: New 30d shows fresh supply. If supply grows faster than removals, competition may increase.
metric_absorption_agent: Absorption compares removed listings to active stock. Higher absorption usually means a hotter segment.
metric_removed_vs_new_agent: Removed vs New helps you see whether stock is shrinking or being replenished. Above 1.0 often means demand is outpacing supply.
metric_velocity_agent: Velocity score compresses speed, removals, and supply balance into one indicator. Use it to quickly rank segments.
metric_median_dom_buyer: If DOM is high, listings sit longer and buyers often gain negotiation room. If it is low, attractive listings can disappear quickly.
metric_removed_30d_buyer: A lot of removals may mean strong demand. In those segments, hesitation can cost you better options.
metric_new_30d_buyer: More new listings can mean more choice. That often improves buyer leverage.
metric_absorption_buyer: High absorption means the market is eating through supply quickly. Low absorption can mean calmer conditions for buyers.
metric_removed_vs_new_buyer: If removals are lower than new supply, buyers may have more room to compare and negotiate.
metric_velocity_buyer: Velocity score helps you quickly judge whether you are entering a hot or slower market.
metric_median_dom_developer: Median DOM helps evaluate how efficiently the market absorbs stock. Faster segments can be more interesting for further feasibility analysis.
metric_removed_30d_developer: Removed 30d gives a recent read on market take-up. It is useful when validating whether a segment is active enough.
metric_new_30d_developer: New supply shows where inventory is entering. Compare it with removals to estimate whether the market can absorb additional product.
metric_absorption_developer: Absorption is one of the strongest quick indicators for supply-demand balance. Higher absorption can suggest healthier take-up.
metric_removed_vs_new_developer: Removed vs New helps spot shrinking vs expanding stock. This is helpful when judging if a segment is tightening or saturating.
metric_velocity_developer: Velocity score is a screening indicator. It should not replace feasibility, but it helps identify segments worth deeper due diligence.
